regime: Low real rates with a flat yield curve
key_drivers: Flat US Treasury yield curve (0.25 pp)
reasoning: The indicators provide conflicting signals. The US Treasury yield curve is currently 'flat' (0.25 pp), which according to rules [aad10797e50f] and [e0d011fb7082] is associated with a transition from expansion to contraction or expectations of economic contraction, signaling a bearish outlook. However, this is countered by the US 10-year real interest rate, which is low relative to history (18th percentile) and falling. According to rules [99ad6cdde172], [2328dabf508c], and [239bc87b8f03], low and decreasing real rates increase liquidity and support expansionary GDP, which is bullish. Furthermore, real rates near zero support higher equity PE multiples [5e068f4bde46] and suppressed volatility [0826c79434b2]. The bullish signal from the liquidity/real rate environment currently outweighs the bearish signal from the flat curve, as the curve is not yet inverted (which would trigger the high-confidence bearish rules [458bf2d7a197] and [92d1d2667cdd]).
